dict.string: Loom reads your Photos library to compose aesthetic walls. Everything stays on this Mac — nothing is uploaded.
Needed only when you pick a folder on your Desktop. Loom reads those photos to build a wall, and doesn't touch anything else.
Needed only when you pick a folder inside Documents. Loom reads those photos to build a wall, and doesn't touch anything else.
Needed only when you pick a folder inside Downloads. Loom reads those photos to build a wall, and doesn't touch anything else.
Needed only when you pick a folder on an external drive. Loom reads those photos to build a wall, and doesn't touch anything else.
Needed only when you pick a folder on a network share. Loom reads those photos to build a wall, and doesn't touch anything else.

Loom uses the camera only when you turn on hand gestures in Settings — to read the openness of your hand and detect a shake-to-shuffle motion. Video is processed in memory frame by frame and never recorded, saved, or sent anywhere.
